title: Emit a BMI calculate row when WEIGHT and HEIGHT both exist on the form
description: When a form captures both WEIGHT (kg) and HEIGHT (cm) fields, emit a calculate row immediately after, with calculation `${WEIGHT} div (${HEIGHT} * ${HEIGHT} div 10000)` and readonly=yes. Unit assumption: weight in kg, height in cm. BMI = kg / m², so the calculation converts cm to m via `div 10000`.
rationale: BMI is the most common derived value in clinical trials and is computed from inputs the form already collects. Pre-computing it removes a manual step (and the typos that come with manual computation) and gives investigators an instant data-quality signal — wildly out-of-range BMI usually points to a typo in height or weight rather than a real outlier.

flag.message: Form ${form.form_id} contains WEIGHT and HEIGHT — verify a BMI calculate row exists with type=calculate (visible if displayed; see OC-5b), name=BMI, label='BMI (kg/m²)', calculation=`${WEIGHT} div (${HEIGHT} * ${HEIGHT} div 10000)`, readonly=yes.

effect.soft: Emit a calculate row immediately after the HEIGHT/WEIGHT row pair: type=calculate, name=BMI, label='BMI (kg/m²)', calculation=`${WEIGHT} div (${HEIGHT} * ${HEIGHT} div 10000)`, readonly=yes. If BMI should be visible in the data-entry view, convert type=calculate to type=text per OC-5b. Row insertion is not a current DSL primitive — handled here in soft.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-7E during Phase B.1c-final. Uses form.has_field DSL operator from B.1c-1 (commit c9ef634). Two form.has_field conditions are wrapped in all_of to avoid JSON key-collision (both have the same top-level key name). Hybrid because the engine cannot currently insert new rows into form.survey — applies_when correctly detects the trigger but the calculate-row emission is delegated to Claude via soft.
